items.title: [正面] The 1.5 Anniversary marks my half-year anniversary...
items.summary:
The 1.5 Anniversary marks my half-year anniversary of starting Morimens and 490 hours playing, I dearly love this game so it's really overdue for a review.  

[h3]This is split into the following: Gacha & Rewards, F2P Friendly, Difficulty, Deckbuilder, Updates, Design & Artwork, Horror Elements, Story & Translation, Steam Version, Anniversary Rewards, TL;DR[/h3]

[b]Gacha & Rewards:[/b] For context, I have played 19 other gacha games and Morimens is the most generous. The amount of rolls you get from the campaign and frequent events is incredible. The pity rates don't require as many pulls compared to other gachas and the devs often give free "reset" vouchers which allows you to reallocate dupe characters into a character of your choice. There's zero materials, currency or characters that you can't earn by playing for free, the [i]only[/i] item locked behind paying is 3 wheel cards which can easily be substituted with another. 

[b]F2P Friendly:[/b] It hurts seeing Morimens called "Pay to Win" because it's simply not. The campaign gives you more than enough rolls to obtain every standard [i]and limited[/i] character-- limited characters are also added to the standard banners over time. If you're familiar with gachas, there is an in-game currency to convert into rolls called Silver Prime, every first clear gives you more! The stats, upgrades and enlightenment are disabled in PVP (the free starting team is actually quite Meta there too) so if luck is on your side and you already have experience with deckbuilders/card-battlers, the PVP mode can give you a massive boost. 
Personally, I have only spent $15 total since playing, this was after 2 months so at that point I didn't even need the upgrades from the monthly pass, I just wanted to try one of the previously mentioned exclusive cards (and after such a heavy time investment, it deserves an extra tip as support) 

[b]Difficulty[/b] I am going to hold your hand when saying this; but if you're really struggling with the stages [i]there is a really powerful revive function[/i], this is completely free to use too! There's "Death Defiance" which prevents death if the value is over 100%, but there's also "Emergency Gnosis" which FULLY restores your health, immune to damage and maxes ultimate energy for your whole team. The only catch is that you must wait 24 hours to recharge emergency revives, however this stacks up to 5 times and can be used multiple times in the same battle. I've played my fair share of "unforgiving" card-battlers and Morimens is easy and very forgiving (did almost every mission under the recommended level), plus the revive function secures your victory, if you do need that extra boost!

[b]Deckbuilder:[/b] I'm a really huge fan of this genre and have played 15 different deckbuilders, I absolutely adore the mechanics introduced here. There's 4 very unique factions with their own dedicated mechanics and aesthetics, you can mix and match any two! This wasn't always the case though and the devs updated every faction to allow them all to work seamlessly together! After you unlock Wheel of Destiny cards and Covenants, this is when careful planning and discovering powerful synergy between characters and gear really comes into play. The PVE gamemode offers a rouge-like experience where you can pickup relics and orisons that modify your stats, mechanics and cards, this is the more traditional "deckbuilding" seen in the genre. Bosses and enemies require different strategies, using a different faction, character or wheel card can make a stage drastically easier-- or similarly turn against you, so you must adapt and plan accordingly! 

[b]Updates:[/b] The devs are [i]incredibly fast[/i] with patches and bug fixes, in the same vein; the size/time to update the game itself is very well optimized and doesn't use much data. The PVP mode is surprisingly balanced and there's not many team comps that you can't win with some extra luck, planning and counters. 

[b]Design & Artwork:[/b] I am ridiculously picky when it comes to character designs and art style, but every aspect is high quality and satisfying to look at! Every character is unique, the lovecraftian horror elements is a massive bonus, while the art style is perfectly consistent and beautiful. Small disclaimer; [spoiler]there is one character that I dislike and think is inappropriate because of how young she is,[/spoiler] but that's only one character out of a massive cast so it's easy to avoid characters you personally don't like-- you can even [i]send them back to the void[/i] if you accidentally pull them! The user interface is clean and quick to understand too, with exact percentages in descriptions for accurate buff increases and auto calculated math on many skills. For example, it will tell you what 30% of the enemy's health is, the final damage after buffs/debuffs and even highlight cards when you can activate the special effect.

[b]Horror Elements:[/b] There's many Lovecraftian and horror tropes that are a delight to encounter, it's [i]refreshing[/i] to have a more mature focused gacha with dark topics that doesn't shy from heartache or angst. That said if you're not a horror fan or sensitive to grim media, you can skip the story and still enjoy the gameplay, which leads to the next point... 

[b]Story & Translation:[/b] Full disclosure that I am not a fan of visual novels in general, I am a chronic skipper for stories told in this format. That said, I have completed both campaigns and read the chapters that were engaging or feature my favorite characters, there have been many times where I've cried! Every character gets their turn in the spotlight and most have a dedicated story mode. Now the elephant in the room; the English translations... Yes, there have been several errors, misfitting words and inconsistencies, but if you can allow some grace for not being the native language, the story is still easy to follow, entertaining and emotional. I have been playing for six months and the translations keep getting better with every update! The card descriptions, story, events, UI, everything, there's [i]constant improvements[/i]. I honestly just appreciate there's an English option at all and it's been clear enough to fully complete the game and memorize every characters' mechanics in both PVE/PVP by heart.

[b]Steam Version:[/b] The mobile version of Morimens is so identical to the steam version that I can't tell which was first and both perform flawlessly. As with any game, your mileage may vary based on device/wifi used, but my phone and laptop runs without any issues, no lag, no glitches, no heating up-- even after playing for 4+ hours straight during events!

[b]Anniversary Rewards:[/b] To end this review, I must emphasize again how generous the rewards are. Currently, with the 1.5 Anniversary Event, you can get 100 free gacha rolls [i]just by logging in[/i]. Free exclusive wheel of destiny card that's given an extra upgrade with each login. A newbie/starter banner which guarantees a character completely free, once per account. A player-wide event mode that allows you to repeatedly challenge a boss up to 20 times each day, which matches your team/level to 60 and you borrow characters from friends to make it even easier. Lastly, four new skins that you can also acquire for free by completing in-game tasks.

[h3][b]TL;DR:[/b] Morimens is the golden standard for what a gacha game should be and brings many unique experiences to the table as a deckbuilder! There is so much to love in every aspect and it's clear the devs genuinely care about the game. It has already evolved a great deal during the time I've been playing, with constant improvements still being added. This is one of the most Free-To-Play gachas out there and you don't need to spend a cent to finish the campaign or reach the top of the leaderboards, just time and skill.[/h3]

items.title: [负面] Очень кривой перевод на русском
[正面] I am trying to rehab myself from gatcha hell, if y...
items.summary:
I am trying to rehab myself from gatcha hell, if you know you know, and I'll be honest with you all, Morimens is SO generous with their pulls and currencies. I've been playing for few months and I've collected 90% of the characters with minimum spending (only when I really REALLY want that character, and even then it is guaranteed earlier, with lesser pull needed.)

I believe they are trying to globalise and make this game more english language friendly, as of now they're using ChatGPT assisted translation (it is doable, and understandable, but some words especially pronouns translated weirdly, since their pronoun in Mandarin, aren't the same gendered as english, or other gendered languages.

Technical aspects aside, this is a VERY good lovecraftian horror, drama game, card based; very macabre storyline about eldrich unknowns, cults, monsters from the beyond, dissolution, annihilation, all that good stuff.

I would give it 9/10. 1 more in the future to be 10/10 once it is fully translated ^_^, but even so, this is amazing already. Thank you to the developers for their efforts thus far.

If you like all that, and also like Reverse 1999-ish aethetics, then this game is for you!

items.title: [正面] The art is so amazing and the story is beautiful, ...
items.summary: The art is so amazing and the story is beautiful, I can confidently say that this is one of the first gacha games who's story I gen don't skip. Please give it a try if you like the art and deep thought provoking storytelling.
